calibration.failure_mode: Recap intro narration. UNKNOWN_SHAPE acceptable per Lesson 2; recap-vocab caught.
Player asked the time, Matt establishes campaign-clock. Textbook cumulative_anchor.
Task duration (carving the umber hulk head). 25 minutes elapsed during a single in-scene action. Textbook in_scene_compression, not cumulative_anchor.
Same turn, 'it is now there on display' — 'it is now' is locative not temporal. Should not have emitted a second record. DEDUP at extraction or ignore.
'it's been there for a while' — describing the campsite age. NOT a campaign-clock anchor, NOT a real time-mention category. Should be unknown_shape or filtered.
'could have been there for more than a week or two' — speculation about how long a SYMBOL has been on the wall. Not a campaign-clock anchor.
'The next few hours go by without incident' — clean overnight/long-rest transition. Textbook scene_transition.
Walking off to the side of the cavern, single in-scene action. NO travel verb in the same clause (it's a few feet of movement, not party travel between locations). Should be in_scene_compression.
Bridge crossing pause. Active scene continues.
'Been awhile since you've used those' — meta DM-banter about player's deck-shuffling card-trick mechanic, NOT campaign-clock. Should be DISCOURSE D3 (table-talk) or unknown_shape.
'About a minute passes... you come back to your senses' — could be either. Defensible either way.
'A moment passes and he kind of drifts back up' — combat micro-beat (dragon prone, drifts up). This is mid-combat. Should be is_combat_state OR D4 reject.
'next week we're gonna try and do a Q&A... we'll stay for an hour or more after' — production OOC, episode break. Should be D1 rejected. FALSE POSITIVE.
Inter-session bridge: party returned to Emon, fan camped two weeks. Should be scene_transition (between-session timeskip), not unknown_shape. v2 patch identified in validation §6.

'within a week's time, the Briarwoods are supposed to be arriving' — future deadline announcement. Genuinely unclassifiable in current taxonomy (would fit deadline_set_by_npc, filed for narrative_pressure_v1).
Same turn, same phrase repeated. Multi-mention rule should not double-count adjacent same-phrase mentions.
NPC dialogue (Laina says 'we've been working here for about six weeks'). is_npc_dialogue_present=true. Per OQ1 fallback flagged not rejected. Genuinely outside Matt-narration scope.
Travel-time speculation discussion ('flying eagle would take two days'). NPC-dialogue-flagged. Not a real campaign-clock advance.
'I run the front... where you guys have stumbled into every single time' — phrase 'five hours' likely from NPC dialogue. NPC-flagged. cumulative_anchor wrong; should be unknown_shape with NPC flag.
'one moment...' then NPC pulls out box. Short transition with anchor. NPC dialogue present but Matt narrates the moment-elapsed.
'It's getting later in the day' — campaign-clock anchor.
Same turn same anchor; 'later in the day' is the same anchor as 'It's getting later in the day'.
Different anchor in same turn: 'pre-noon, or around noon' — describes when business HAPPENS, slight stretch but acceptable as cumulative_anchor.
'A few minutes go by. Sherri comes back' — clean short transition. NPC follows but Matt-narrated time elapsed.
'A few moments later, she comes back down' — short transition, anchored to prior 'darts up the stairway'.
NPC dialogue (Gilmore quoting deal terms). Should be unknown_shape with NPC flag, not cumulative_anchor.
NPC dialogue. Same turn. Should be unknown_shape.
Third record on the same turn for the SAME phrase 'a month' repeated. Multi-mention bug.

Spell duration mechanic ('Fly is concentration spell, amulet keeps it up for two rounds, which is 12 seconds'). NOT travel_duration. D2 spell-rules-duration should fire OR is_combat_state. Categorically wrong.
NPC backstory ('over the past 1,000 years'). Genuinely unclassifiable in v1. NPC-flagged.
'it's getting close to dusk' — clean campaign-clock anchor.
'It's been crafted for you' — present-perfect crafting, NOT a time anchor. Should not have emitted as cumulative_anchor.
NPC dialogue (smith says 'about two days'). Future task-time estimate, not a campaign-clock advance. NPC-flagged correctly.
Continuation of NPC dialogue. Same.
'a day of work' — describing what an NPC was doing today, idiomatic. NOT a campaign-clock advance. v2 idiom filter or unknown_shape.
'It will take you about 30 minutes' from Vax's current location to the keep. PARTY TRAVEL between locations. travel_duration not in_scene_compression.
'It's been used for a long time' — describes the bed, not campaign-clock. Same shape as C1E003_t458.
'A couple moments later, a voice comes from the other side' — short transition with anchor (NPC knock at door).

'rest up for the next morning, and at that point we're going to take our break. Five minutes, guys' — Matt mixes scene_transition framing with break announcement. D1 should reject the whole turn (locked §11.5). v2 patch: D1 whole-turn vs phrase-span (Lesson 9 candidate).
Production OOC ('strict five minutes break'). FALSE POSITIVE. Should be D1 reject.
Same turn same break-time. Duplicate FP.
'As you wake the next morning' — clean overnight transition.
Same turn. Multi-mention duplicate.
'As you wake up' continues the same morning narration. Either dedup or accept as scene continuation.
'it's getting pretty thick now' — describing facial hair length. NOT a time anchor. False positive on the 'it's getting' pattern.
'you swear for a minute, you see' — combat micro-beat-adjacent but real in-scene moment of dwarf seeing the amulet. Defensible as in_scene_compression.
NPC dialogue ('better part of a year to enchant'). Future task-time NPC quote. NPC-flagged correctly.
'It's been a long time since you've been in this portion of the world' — Matt-narration about elapsed campaign time. Acceptable cumulative_anchor.
Same turn, same anchor 'It's been a long time since you've been'. Duplicate.
NPC dialogue ('since you've left, we haven't had...'). NPC-flagged correctly. cumulative_anchor wrong.
'It takes him about five minutes' counting coins. NPC-flagged but Matt-narrated. In-scene task duration.
'It takes about ten minutes before the keep doors open' — task duration.
'arriving now in six days' — future deadline, not a campaign-clock advance. deadline_set_by_npc-shape, filed for narrative_pressure_v1.
Overnight rest. 'morning sun rises... you come to consciousness'.
Multi-day construction montage. Defensible as scene_transition or in_scene_compression. Either acceptable.
Same turn same phrase 'over the next few days'. Multi-mention dup.
'a few minutes' for the storm to gather. In-scene.
'stunned for a round' — combat mechanic. is_combat_state should fire (it doesn't due to OQ5 25-turn limit). Per Lesson 2 unknown_shape acceptable but is_combat_state would be better.
'morning has come... you have a few hours' — overnight transition into pre-feast prep window.
'as the sun begins to bring itself to the other side' — sunset transition to the feast.
NPC dialogue (Asum: 'arriving around dusk'). NPC-flagged. Should NOT be cumulative_anchor.
Same NPC dialogue continuation.
NPC dialogue (Asum) about feast schedule.
NPC dialogue / backstory recall.
NPC dialogue (Asum coordinating).
NPC dialogue / instructions to staff.
Matt narration: 'I flirt with her throughout the hour' is player; the next-hour narration is Matt's. Should be in_scene_compression with the flirting montage.
NPC dialogue speculation about Allura's time off.
NPC dialogue (Briarwoods). NOT a scene_transition. Should be unknown_shape.
NPC backstory dialogue.
NPC dialogue. travel_duration wrong. Should be unknown_shape with NPC flag.
Same NPC dialogue continuation, different mention.
'it's been completed' — present-perfect about an item, not time-elapsed. False positive.

'It takes a minute' for spell-casting.
'A moment later, they all return with a spectral cow' — short anchored transition.
'It's been a while since you wrestled a bear, so it's kind of fun' — meta-comment about player familiarity, not campaign-clock.
Same turn same phrase shape.
Broom adjustment, in-scene task.
'a few moments pass' is a restatement of the 'few minutes' pattern in the same turn. Multi-mention dup of same fictional moment.
'a few hours into the evening, Keyleth, you are just starting to doze off' — overnight scene transition. Defensible as cumulative_anchor too.
NPC dialogue (Cerkonos).
Vax-dream-sequence narration. Defensible cumulative_anchor or unknown_shape — accept either.

Same turn dream-content reference.

Quadruple-fired same turn.
Quintuple-fired same turn — 5 records on one turn. Egregious multi-mention bug.
'It's been a long time since then' referring to Marisha's character's lack of memory of trees. Meta-recall, not campaign-clock.
Travel-duration estimate (player asking about destination). NOT a campaign-clock advance — this is hypothetical travel-time discussion.
Same turn restating same travel estimate.
Spell-doorway opening; in-scene transition through plant magic, defensible. Could also be travel_duration but the in-scene framing is clean.
Hypothetical travel-time discussion ('on horseback... on foot...') — not a campaign-clock advance.
Same turn same phrase repeated.
Same turn 4th repetition. Multi-mention bug egregious.
Same turn 5th.

NPC dialogue (Reginald).
NPC dialogue (Reginald). NOT travel_duration.
NPC dialogue (Reginald). NOT in_scene_compression.
Backstory recall — describing how someone changed years later. NOT a scene_transition.
NPC dialogue 'let me get loose here for a minute' — idiomatic 'a minute', not actual time. Should be filtered.
NPC 'since you mention it' or similar idiom. The phrase 'since' here doesn't anchor time.
NPC dialogue ('been half a day since breakfast'). NPC-flagged.
Same turn same NPC line.
Validation §6 already flagged this. Fragmented narration about buildings still standing — possibly post-Thordak-attack but not a clear time anchor.
Phrase 'it has been' likely refers to the Margrave's house location/role, not time. Need full context but appears non-time.
'It is quite a busy time in the afternoon' — campaign-clock anchor. Recap-flag is wrong though (it's not actually recap; first scene of the episode but not retelling past).
NPC dialogue, recap-flagged. Real backstory.

NPC dialogue idiom 'one day' = 'someday'.
'after about five minutes or so' grinding spear-tip. Task duration.

'It takes you a few hours and gets you into the early parts of the evening' — task duration.
'As the sun begins to set in Vasselheim... night goes dark' — sunset transition.
'most of it has been turned black through soot' — present-perfect about state of nightgown, NOT time. False positive.
'It has been steeped way too long' — tea steeping comment. Borderline; defensible cumulative_anchor too.
NPC future-speculation idiom.
'it's early afternoon' - clean clock anchor.

'as the sun sets' - sunset transition to dinner.
